name: Nextflow
metaDescription: Nextflow is a workflow language and engine for building scalable, reproducible data pipelines that run unchanged on local machines, HPC, cloud, and Kubernetes.
overview: Nextflow is a workflow system for creating scalable, portable, and reproducible pipelines. It is based on a dataflow programming model, where you describe your work as a set of processes connected by channels of data, and Nextflow handles running those processes in parallel and in the right order.

It is built around a domain-specific language (DSL) for data-driven computational pipelines. You can combine existing scripts and tools written in different languages inside a pipeline without rewriting them, so it is widely used for genomics and bioinformatics work where many separate tools are chained together.

The same pipeline runs unchanged across many environments. Nextflow separates the pipeline logic from the execution layer, so it can run on a local machine, HPC batch schedulers, cloud batch services, and Kubernetes, using containers or package managers to supply software dependencies.
features: Dataflow programming model where parallelism is defined implicitly through process declarations connected by channels
Runs the same pipeline on a local machine, HPC schedulers, cloud batch services, and Kubernetes
Supports HPC batch schedulers including SLURM, LSF, PBS, HTCondor, and GridEngine
Runs on cloud platforms including AWS Batch, Azure Batch, and Google Cloud Batch
Manages software dependencies through containers and package managers including Docker, Podman, Singularity, Conda, and Spack
Continuous checkpoints track intermediate results so a pipeline can resume from the last successful step

gettingStarted.intro: Install Nextflow with the install script, confirm it works, then run a demo pipeline from the terminal.

steps.heading: Check Java
steps.body: Nextflow requires Java 17 or later. Check the installed version before installing.

steps.heading: Install Nextflow
steps.body: Download Nextflow with the install script, which creates the nextflow executable in the current directory, then make it executable.

steps.heading: Verify the installation
steps.body: Confirm Nextflow is installed correctly. You can move the executable to a directory in your PATH to run it from anywhere.

steps.heading: Run a demo pipeline
steps.body: Run the built-in hello pipeline to check that Nextflow can execute a workflow end to end.

useCases: Build genomics and bioinformatics pipelines that chain together many separate command-line tools
Run the same analysis pipeline on a laptop, an HPC cluster, or a cloud batch service without rewriting it
Package each step in a container with Docker or Singularity so runs are reproducible across machines
Resume a long-running pipeline from the last successful step after a failure using continuous checkpoints
